findings.summary: Subscribe docstring describes exact-type check as `type(event) is event_type` but implementation uses dict lookup.
findings.detail: Line 73 docstring: 'checked via type(event) is event_type'. Actual dispatch (line 108) is `self._typed.get(type(event), [])`. The behavior is IDENTICAL (dict hashing on types is identity-based, and StageStarted is not a key when BonfireEvent is registered) so the stated contract holds, but the literal expression cited in the docstring does not appear in the code. Matches V1 verbatim; flagging as V1-inherited micro-nit, not a blocker.
findings.suggestion: Optional post-merge: amend docstring to 'dispatched via dict lookup on type(event), which gives exact-type semantics (subclasses do not trigger parent handlers)'. No code change.

findings.summary: CostTracker re-enters bus.emit from inside a DispatchCompleted handler; sequence numbers for outer and nested events interleave.
findings.detail: When cost crosses 80% / 100%, CostTracker._on_dispatch_completed awaits self._bus.emit(CostBudgetWarning/Exceeded) BEFORE the outer emit's typed loop completes. The nested emit increments the global sequence counter, so subsequent typed handlers for the outer DispatchCompleted will see an outer event stamped with N while a nested Warning carries N+1. This is by design (the outer stamp is frozen at emit() entry and not reassigned) but is worth an architecture note. No test exercises a 'CostTracker first, SessionLogger second' subscription order to lock the contract — the canonical suite covers the externally visible behavior adequately, though. Matches V1 verbatim. No action required.
findings.suggestion: Consider a future doc note in bus.py acknowledging that handlers may re-enter emit and that the outer event's stamp is invariant across the handler's lifetime.

summary.lens_1_test_impl_fidelity: PASS — 112 canonical tests cover 448 LOC across 10 tier clusters (imports, construction, typed subscribe, exact-type filter, global subscribe, C7 ordering, sequential execution, sequence stamping, consumer isolation, edge cases, long session, concrete event flow, dependency constraints). Every major invariant has a dedicated test; mutations that weaken any invariant would fail at least one test. No accidental passes detected.
summary.lens_2_sdk_integration: PASS — grep for claude_agent_sdk|anthropic|claude-code in src/bonfire/events/ returns zero. bus.py imports only stdlib + bonfire.models.events. Explicit TestDependencyConstraints enforces allowlist via AST walk. No Claude Agent SDK leak.
summary.lens_3_c7_ordering: PASS — 4 dedicated C7 tests: typed-before-global with global-registered-first (hardest case), interleaved registration (t1,g1,t2,g2 → t1,t2,g1,g2), typed registration order preserved, global registration order preserved. Implementation dispatches typed loop (lines 107-117) before global loop (lines 119-129).
summary.lens_4_consumer_isolation: PASS — each handler in try/except, logger.exception on failure, emit never re-raises. 7 isolation tests cover: first-raises-second-fires, later-raises-earlier-ran, typed-exception-does-not-block-global, global-exception-does-not-block-peer, emit-never-raises-if-all-fail, handler-exception-is-logged (via caplog), counter-advances-on-failure.
summary.lens_5_sequence_stamping: PASS — monotonic counter, model_copy(update={'sequence': n}), first emit stamps 1, original frozen event preserved (test_original_event_is_not_mutated), counter advances on handler failure, caller-provided sequence is overwritten, all handlers receive same stamp, other fields preserved (test_stamped_copy_preserves_other_fields includes event_id identity check). 8 stamping tests.
summary.lens_6_vault_stub: PASS — in-file minimal stub (71 LOC), 4 typed subscriptions to {StageCompleted, StageFailed, DispatchFailed, SessionEnded} via register(bus), backend.store() wrapped in try/except with logger.warning. No bonfire.vault.* imports. Surface contract tests (TestVaultIngestConsumerSurface) lock only what W2.3 owns; deep semantics explicitly deferred per Sage §2.
summary.lens_7_wire_consumers: PASS — signature `wire_consumers(*, bus, persistence, cost_tracker, display_callback, vault_backend) -> None`. `*` enforces keyword-only. test_wire_consumers_is_keyword_only asserts positional call raises TypeError. test_wire_consumers_accepts_all_five_kwargs + 4 integration tests verify wiring outcomes. V1's persona + cost_ledger_path correctly omitted (documented adaptation).

v1_cross_check.consumers/display.py: Adapted — PersonaProtocol type replaced with Any (module absent in public v0.1). Behavior identical.
v1_cross_check.consumers/logger.py: Adapted — SessionPersistence type replaced with Any (module absent in public v0.1). Behavior identical.
v1_cross_check.consumers/vault_ingest.py: Sage-approved Option A substitution. V1 is a 5-LOC re-export from bonfire.vault.consumer; public v0.1 inlines the minimal stub since bonfire.vault does not yet exist.
v1_cross_check.consumers/__init__.py: Adapted — wire_consumers drops persona + cost_ledger_path kwargs; removes CostLedgerConsumer wiring (bonfire.costs absent). Sage §3 adjudication.

invariants.test_scrub: only public ticket BON-333 appears in test docstrings — acceptable provenance
